q.en: What is this book mainly about?
q.zh: 这本书主要讲的是什么？

options.en: Jupiter, the largest planet in our solar system, and its many surprising features
options.zh: 木星——太阳系中最大的行星，以及它许多令人惊讶的特征

options.en: How to build your own telescope at home
options.zh: 如何在家自己制作望远镜

options.en: The history of ancient Roman gods
options.zh: 古罗马诸神的历史

options.en: Why Earth is the brightest object in the night sky
options.zh: 为什么地球是夜空中最亮的天体

explain.en: The book explores Jupiter's size, composition, weather, moons, rings, and spacecraft visits, showing it is full of surprises.
explain.zh: 全书围绕木星的尺寸、组成、天气、卫星、光环和探测任务展开，说明它充满惊喜。

q.en: Where is Jupiter located in relation to the Sun and other planets?
q.zh: 木星相对于太阳和其他行星处于什么位置？

options.en: It is the fifth planet from the Sun, between Mars and Saturn
options.zh: 它是距离太阳第五远的行星，位于火星和土星之间

options.en: It is the third planet from the Sun, next to Earth
options.zh: 它是距离太阳第三远的行星，紧邻地球

options.en: It is the closest planet to the Sun
options.zh: 它是离太阳最近的行星

options.en: It is the farthest planet from the Sun
options.zh: 它是离太阳最远的行星

explain.en: Jupiter is the fifth planet from the Sun, orbiting in between Mars and Saturn, about 484 million miles from the Sun.
explain.zh: 木星是距离太阳第五远的行星，在火星和土星之间运行，距太阳约4.84亿英里。

q.en: What is Jupiter mostly made of?

options.en: A big ball of gases and liquids, mostly hydrogen and helium
options.zh: 一个巨大的气体与液体球，主要是氢和氦

options.en: A solid rocky surface you can walk on
options.zh: 可以行走的坚硬岩石表面

options.en: Mostly liquid water like Earth's oceans
options.zh: 主要像地球海洋一样的液态水

options.en: Solid ice and frozen gases only
options.zh: 只有固态冰和冻结的气体

explain.en: Unlike Earth, Jupiter has no solid surface; it is mostly gases and liquids, especially hydrogen and helium.
explain.zh: 与地球不同，木星没有固体表面，主要由气体和液体构成，尤其是氢和氦。

q.en: The author compares Jupiter's size to Earth by saying: 'if Earth were a small grape, Jupiter would be a large grapefruit.' What does this comparison help show?
q.zh: 作者用“如果地球是一颗小葡萄，木星就是一颗大葡萄柚”来比较两星大小。这种比喻是为了说明什么？

options.en: That Jupiter is enormously larger than Earth
options.zh: 木星比地球大得多

options.en: That Earth and Jupiter are exactly the same size
options.zh: 地球和木星大小完全一样

options.en: That Jupiter is smaller than Earth
options.zh: 木星比地球还小

options.en: That grapes grow on Jupiter
options.zh: 木星上长葡萄

explain.en: The simile helps readers picture how much bigger Jupiter is than Earth using familiar fruits.
explain.zh: 这个比喻用熟悉的水果帮助读者想象木星比地球大出多少。

q.en: What is the Great Red Spot on Jupiter?
q.zh: 木星上的“大红斑”是什么？

options.en: A giant storm that has been raging for more than 300 years
options.zh: 一场已经持续了300多年的巨型风暴

options.en: A frozen lake of water on the surface
options.zh: 表面上一个结冰的水湖

options.en: The name of one of Jupiter's moons
options.zh: 木星其中一颗卫星的名字

options.en: A crater made by a falling asteroid
options.zh: 由小行星撞击形成的陨石坑

explain.en: The Great Red Spot is a huge storm, big enough for three Earths to fit across, that has lasted over 300 years.
explain.zh: 大红斑是一场巨大的风暴，宽度足以横跨三个地球，已持续超过300年。

q.en: Who discovered Jupiter's four largest moons, and when?
q.zh: 木星的四大卫星是谁在何时发现的？

options.en: The astronomer Galileo Galilei, in 1610
options.zh: 天文学家伽利略·伽利雷，于1610年

options.en: The author, with his telescope in 1985
options.zh: 作者在1985年用他的望远镜

options.en: NASA astronauts in 1990
options.zh: NASA宇航员在1990年

options.en: Ancient Roman astronomers over 2,000 years ago
options.zh: 2000多年前的古罗马天文学家

explain.en: The four largest moons (Io, Europa, Ganymede, Callisto) were discovered by Galileo Galilei in 1610.
explain.zh: 四颗最大的卫星（伊奥、欧罗巴、盖尼米德、卡利斯托）是伽利略·伽利雷在1610年发现的。

q.en: The book says Europa may have an ocean of warm water under its icy surface, and scientists think alien creatures may live there. What does this show about how readers should combine ideas from the text?
q.zh: 书中说欧罗巴冰层下可能有温暖的海洋，科学家认为那里或许有外星生物。这提示读者应如何整合文中的信息？

options.en: Readers can connect the idea of a hidden ocean with the possibility of life on another moon
options.zh: 读者可以把“隐藏的海洋”与“另一颗卫星上可能存在生命”联系起来

options.en: The book proves that aliens have already been found on Europa
options.zh: 书中证明欧罗巴上已经发现了外星人

options.en: Europa is made entirely of solid rock
options.zh: 欧罗巴完全由坚硬的岩石构成

options.en: Only Earth can possibly have water
options.zh: 只有地球才可能有水

explain.en: The text links the hidden ocean beneath Europa's ice to the possibility of life, asking readers to combine those ideas.
explain.zh: 文中把欧罗巴冰层下的海洋与生命的可能性联系起来，要求读者整合这两条信息。

q.en: When and how were Jupiter's rings discovered?
q.zh: 木星的光环是在何时、如何被发现的？

options.en: In 1979, by cameras on the Voyager spacecraft flying near Jupiter
options.zh: 1979年，由靠近木星飞行的旅行者号航天器上的相机发现

options.en: In 1610, by Galileo looking through a telescope
options.zh: 1610年，伽利略用望远镜观测发现

options.en: In 1990, by the Hubble Space Telescope
options.zh: 1990年，由哈勃太空望远镜发现

options.en: Thousands of years ago by ancient Romans
options.zh: 数千年前由古罗马人发现

explain.en: Jupiter's thin rings were first photographed in 1979 by cameras on the Voyager spacecraft.
explain.zh: 木星细窄的光环于1979年首次由旅行者号航天器上的相机拍摄到。

q.en: Which spacecraft was the FIRST to actually orbit Jupiter?
q.zh: 哪一艘航天器是第一个真正环绕木星运行的？

options.en: Galileo, which orbited Jupiter from 1995 to 2003
options.zh: 伽利略号，它在1995至2003年间环绕木星运行

options.en: Pioneer 10, which took the first close-up photos in 1973
options.zh: 先驱者10号，它在1973年拍下首批近距离照片

options.en: Voyager 1, which discovered the rings in 1979
options.zh: 旅行者1号，它在1979年发现了光环

options.en: The Hubble Space Telescope, placed in orbit in 1990
options.zh: 哈勃太空望远镜，1990年被送入轨道

explain.en: Galileo was the first spacecraft to orbit Jupiter, circling it from 1995 to 2003 and even dropping a probe into its atmosphere.
explain.zh: 伽利略号是首个环绕木星的航天器，1995至2003年间绕其运行，并向大气投放了探测器。

q.en: Why does the author begin the book with his own story of sketching Jupiter in 1985?
q.zh: 作者为什么要用自己1985年画木星素描的经历作为开篇？

options.en: To show his personal excitement and interest, drawing readers into the topic
options.zh: 为了表达他个人的兴奋与兴趣，把读者引入主题

options.en: To teach readers how to draw planets professionally
options.zh: 为了教读者如何专业地画行星

options.en: To prove he is a better artist than scientist
options.zh: 为了证明他画画比做科学更好

options.en: Because the book is a work of fiction
options.zh: 因为这本书是虚构作品

explain.en: The author shares a personal, surprising experience to hook readers and reveal his wonder at Jupiter.
explain.zh: 作者分享自己惊讶的亲身经历，是为了吸引读者并表达他对木星的惊叹。

prompt.en: The author watched Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 crash into Jupiter in 1994 and saw Jupiter's stripes change before his eyes. Imagine you had a telescope and saw one of these events. What would you feel, and what would you most want to observe or learn about next?
prompt.zh: 作者在1994年目睹了舒梅克-列维9号彗星撞上木星，也曾看到木星的条纹在眼前变化。想象你也有一台望远镜并亲眼看到其中一幕，你会有什么感受？你接下来最想观察或了解什么？

en: Think about why watching a changing planet feels exciting or surprising.
Connect your curiosity to something specific in the book, like the moons, the Great Red Spot, or a spacecraft mission.
zh: 想一想，亲眼看到一颗变化的行星为何让人兴奋或惊讶。
把你的问题和书中具体的某一点联系起来，比如卫星、大红斑或某次探测任务。
